title: Evangelion InterCharacter Relations No Spoilers
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion InterCharacter Relations [No Spoilers!]             Quote Post by birdyofthemoongoddess » Fri Jan 29, 2010 9:09 pm As I was watching episode 21 and caught on to all the lust/love/yearnings there were revealed, I realized something. I was actually quite surprised; I hadn't expected to find such a tactic implemented in a show adored and obsessed over by so many. Who's idea was it to include plot lines overused by and associated with 20 year old soap dramas and yaoi? Did anyone notice how unrealistic and relatively cliche it is to have every single character have romantic feelings for another known character ? For Example: Shinji - while relatively unsexual and awkward around girls, there's no denying he feels some form of attraction towards Asuka, at the least. Misato - dated Kaji while younger, then rekindled her true feelings with him shortly before he was killed. Rei - (previously was: "due to being part of Yui, Rei held an odd attraction towards Gendo; Yet, as an imcomplete soul (that's how I think of it, anyway), she really couldn't comprehend emotions to their fullest." But, I suppose I've been proven wrong.) Fix'd to: ... Gendo? >.> Asuka - wanted Kaji; wanted Shinji (as much as she denied it) Gendo - married Yui, fooled around with Akagi? (right?) Ritsuko - I feel that she's a closet lesbian. I mean, in 21 she makes that comment about how man/woman relationships are unnatural ("You can never tell with a man and a woman because it's not logical" are her exact words) and she says something else about how Misato changed after she and Kaji started dating. I'm guessing she had a thing for Misato. Yui - married Gendo. And, seeing as Rei has some weird-ass feelings for the guy, Yui must've before she died. Fuyutsuki - fell for Yui (I love how in 21 there's that scene where he's talking with her, and it keeps showing her boobs and chest. XD made me laugh) Kaji - dated Misato (most likely truly loved her) Kaworu - while, no, I have not yet met him in the series yet, he so wants Shinji. Akagi - was jealous of Yui because she liked Gendo; fooled around with him (at least kissed him) after Yui died Maya - had a crush on Ritsuko Aoba - (previously was: "had a crush on Maya" but I've also been yelled at for this one so... ) Fix'd to: an exception. Hyuga - had a crush on Misato Toji - Another exception. Fix'd with: While Hikari liked him it is unclear whether he knows/feels the same towards her Hikari - adored Toji. Kensuke - found Misato attractive, at the least. ... Maybe it just doesn't bother anyone? Maybe the overall 'mind-blowing' ness and complexity of the series allows this relatively unimportant 'flaw' to be overlooked? But, I feel like this would get on the nerves of more people. Does it get on your nerves? Or do you just not care? Or, do you find that it adds to the story? ( In my opinion, I find it a tad bit overdone, but I'm also a hopeless romantic and enjoy the complexity it brings to the plot. ) Discuss! (please > <)

[]: Re: Evangelion IntrerCharacter Relations             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Fri Jan 29, 2010 9:13 pm birdyofthemoongoddess wrote: Aoba - has a crush on Maya

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Jan 29, 2010 9:19 pm There are four main basins of attraction in Evangelion. They are 1) Yui 2) Shinji 3) Misato 4) Gendou This post is not a joke.

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Fri Jan 29, 2010 9:28 pm Most of the characters tend to have a repressed sexual attraction to Pen^2. All of the other lustful/love relationships can be traced to a transference of Eros from him to whatever character the subject is lusting after.

[]: Re: Evangelion InterCharacter Relations             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Jan 29, 2010 9:30 pm birdyofthemoongoddess wrote: Ritsuko - I feel that she's a closet lesbian. I mean, in 21 she makes that comment about how man/woman relationships are unnatural ("You can never tell with a man and a woman because it's not logical" are her exact words) and she says something else about how Misato changed after she and Kaji started dating. I'm guessing she had a thing for Misato.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Jan 29, 2010 10:24 pm Without going into series spoilers, you'll find out what Ritsuko's deal is in 23'. Also, I really hope you'll watch the DC episodes because they help shed light on certain events in EoE. (Speaking of which, when you're done with the series and want to watch Death & EoE (fansubbed, remasted Renewal quality ) send me a PM, I have a good way for us to do that.) By the way, Toji seemed to think Misato was attractive too, and it's ambiguous whether or not he knew Hikari liked him or felt the same way as her. Personally, I like the complexity character relationships bring to the plot, and being a romantic at heart, it doesn't bother me at all (though there are certain pairings or shipping I prefer to others from the series).

[]:             Quote Post by JoeD80 » Fri Jan 29, 2010 11:53 pm Doesn't bother me; intercharacter relations and sex and romance and unrequited love are part of the story. It's definitely part of the appeal of the show to me. It's hard for a story like that to work if your characters are attracted to people off-screen.

[]: Re: Evangelion InterCharacter Relations [No Spoilers!]             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Jan 30, 2010 3:35 am birdyofthemoongoddess wrote: Did anyone notice how unrealistic and relatively cliche it is to have every single character have romantic feelings for another known character ?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Jan 30, 2010 3:41 am Did anyone notice how unrealistic and relatively cliche it is to have every single character have romantic feelings for another known character?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Jan 30, 2010 3:45 am I don't think it's a stretch. Nerv operates with a very close-knit group for secutiry/secrecy reasons, with many of them selected primarily -because- of these pre-existing relationships. On the other side you've got a group of people working together under a ton of stress, putting their lives in each other' hands and stuck in near isolation most of the time, so relationships are practically guaranteed to spring up simply as a stress-release valve.

[]: Re: Evangelion InterCharacter Relations [No Spoilers!]             Quote Post by Synapsid » Sat Jan 30, 2010 4:12 am birdyofthemoongoddess wrote: As I was watching episode 21 and caught on to all the lust/love/yearnings there were revealed, I realized something. I was actually quite surprised; I hadn't expected to find such a tactic implemented in a show adored and obsessed over by so many. Who's idea was it to include plot lines overused by and associated with 20 year old soap dramas and yaoi? Did anyone notice how unrealistic and relatively cliche it is to have every single character have romantic feelings for another known character ?

[]:             Quote Post by birdyofthemoongoddess » Sat Jan 30, 2010 11:04 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: I really hope you'll watch the DC episodes because they help shed light on certain events in EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Jan 30, 2010 3:31 pm But, it seems that in every other series I watch where something along these lines happens (where all the characters like each other) people bitch and moan constantly over it. (take Gravitation for example.)

[]:             Quote Post by Halicat » Fri Feb 05, 2010 8:04 am I agree with what Auratwilight said, considering Eva is all about human relationships and feelings, the way the characters feel for each other makes for some of the most crucial aspects of the show [I mean for example, without Gendo's obbession with getting Yui back we wouldn't have Rei, or Shinj's probmatic relationship with him or even EoE and Misato and Shinji would never have found out the truth behind Rei [And therefore, the viewer wouldn't have] if it weren't for Ritsuko's breakdown]
